      <title>Assailants kill at least 32 in north-west Nigerian villages, residents say</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/14/assailants-kill-raids-northwest-nigeria-villages-residents-say</link>
      <description><p>Residents who escaped violence tell of bandits riding in on motorbikes and shooting indiscriminately</p><p>Armed assailants on motorbikes killed at least 32 people and burned houses and shops during raids on three villages in north-west Nigeria’s Niger state early on Saturday, local officials and residents who escaped the violence said.</p><p>The dawn raids targeted the communities of Tunga-Makeri, Konkoso, and Pissa.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/14/assailants-kill-raids-northwest-nigeria-villages-residents-say">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>53 people dead or missing after migrant boat capsizes in Mediterranean</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/09/deaths-migrant-boat-capsizes-mediterranean-libya</link>
      <description><p>Only two survivors rescued after boat overturned off Libyan coast, UN migration agency says</p><p>Fifty-three people are dead or missing after a boat capsized in the Mediterranean Sea off the Libyan coast, the UN migration agency said on Monday. Only two survivors were rescued.</p><p>The International Organization for Migration said the boat overturned north of Zuwara on Friday, in the latest disaster involving people attempting the perilous Mediterranean crossing in the hope of reaching Europe.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/09/deaths-migrant-boat-capsizes-mediterranean-libya">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>Ebo Taylor, Ghanaian highlife pioneer and guitarist, dies age 90</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/music/2026/feb/09/ebo-taylor-ghanaian-highlife-pioneer-dies-age-90</link>
      <description><p>Taylor, who did for Ghanaian music what his friend Fela Kuti did for Nigeria, has been called the greatest rhythm guitarist in history</p><p>Ghanaian musician Ebo Taylor, a definitive force behind the highlife genre, has died age 90.</p><p>His son Kweku Taylor announced the news on Sunday: “The world has lost a giant. A colossus of African music. Ebo Taylor passed away yesterday; a day after the launch of Ebo Taylor music festival and exactly a month after his 90th birthday, leaving behind an unmatched artistry legacy. Dad, your light will never fade.”</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/music/2026/feb/09/ebo-taylor-ghanaian-highlife-pioneer-dies-age-90">Continue reading...</a></description>

    <item>
      <title>Weather tracker: Spain and Portugal hit by third deadly storm in two weeks</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/environment/2026/feb/09/weather-tracker-spain-and-portugal-hit-by-third-fatal-storm</link>
      <description><p>Storm Marta sweeps Iberian peninsula just days after Storms Kristin and Leonardo brought deadly flooding and major damage</p><p>Spain and Portugal have endured another storm over the weekend, just days after the deadly flooding and major damage caused by Storm Kristin and Storm Leonardo last week. Storm Marta passed over the Iberian peninsula on Saturday, bringing fresh torrential rain and killing two people. Storm Kristin killed at least five people after it made landfall on 28 January with <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/environment/2026/feb/06/weather-tracker-storm-leonardo-europe-portugal-spain-northern-africa">Storm Leonardo claiming another victim last Wednesday</a>.</p><p>The outlook for this week is for more rain across Spain, Portugal and France, especially across north-west Portugal, where more than 100mm is possible during the first half of the week. Some of the heaviest of the rain will transfer to southern Italy and western parts of Greece and Turkey later in the week.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/environment/2026/feb/09/weather-tracker-spain-and-portugal-hit-by-third-fatal-storm">Continue reading...</a></description>

    <item>
      <title>RSF drone attack kills 24 people fleeing fighting in central Sudan, says doctors group</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/07/sudan-rsf-drone-attack-rahad-kills-displaced-people</link>
      <description><p>Eight children including two infants among dead in vehicle carrying displaced people, says Sudan Doctors Network</p><p>A drone attack by a paramilitary group has hit a vehicle carrying displaced families in central Sudan, killing at least 24 people, including eight children, a doctors’ group said on Saturday.</p><p>The attack by the Rapid Support Forces took place close to the city of Er Rahad in North Kordofan province, according to the Sudan Doctors Network, which tracks the country’s war. The vehicle was transporting displaced people who fled fighting in the Dubeiker area, the group said in a statement. Among the dead children were two infants.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/07/sudan-rsf-drone-attack-rahad-kills-displaced-people">Continue reading...</a></description>

    <item>
      <title>‘People want to help’: Canadians rally round Tumbler Ridge after school shooting</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/15/canada-support-tumbler-ridge-school-shooting</link>
      <description><p>Tragedy has prompted a wave of support for town from neighbouring communities and across country</p><p>When Jim Caruso heard the news of the school shooting in Tumbler Ridge, he knew immediately he needed to be there. He packed his bags and boarded a plane for the community 700 miles away. “I wanted to be here to bring some level of comfort,” he said. “I wanted to hug people, pray for them and, most importantly, to cry with them.”</p><p>On Tuesday, a shooter opened fire in the town’s secondary school, <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/11/canada-school-shooting-ten-dead-including-suspect-in-attack-on-tumbler-ridge-secondary-school-and-residence-police-say">killing eight people</a>, most of them young children. It was one of the deadliest attacks in Canada’s history and has left the country reeling.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/15/canada-support-tumbler-ridge-school-shooting">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>US forces board second Venezuela‑linked oil tanker in Indian Ocean</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/15/us-forces-board-second-venezuelalinked-oil-tanker-in-indian-ocean</link>
      <description><p>Pentagon tracked sanctioned Veronica III from Caribbean Sea after it left Venezuela on day Maduro was captured</p><p>US military forces boarded another sanctioned tanker in the Indian Ocean after tracking the vessel from the Caribbean Sea in an effort to target illicit oil connected to Venezuela, the Pentagon said on Sunday.</p><p>Venezuela had faced US sanctions on its oil for several years, relying on a shadow fleet of falsely flagged tankers to smuggle crude into global supply chains. Donald Trump ordered a quarantine of sanctioned tankers in December to pressure the president, Nicolás Maduro, before Maduro was apprehended in January during a US military operation.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/15/us-forces-board-second-venezuelalinked-oil-tanker-in-indian-ocean">Continue reading...</a></description>

    <item>
      <title>No fuel, no tourists, no cash – this was the week the Cuban crisis got real</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/news/ng-interactive/2026/feb/15/cuba-crisis-oil-shortage-venezuela-donald-trump-havana</link>
      <description><p>Diplomats in Havana are preparing for an alternative Trump tactic: the country being starved until people take to the streets and the US can step in</p><p>Among the verdant gardens of Havana’s diplomatic quarter, Siboney, ambassadors from countries traditionally allied to the United States are expressing increasing frustration with Washington’s attempt to unseat Cuba’s government, while simultaneously drawing up plans to draw down their missions.</p><p>Cuba is in crisis. Already reeling from a four-year economic slump, worsened by hyper-inflation and the migration of nearly 20% of the population, the 67-year-old communist government is at its weakest. After Washington’s successful military operation against Cuba’s ally Venezuela at the beginning of January, the US administration is actively seeking regime change.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/news/ng-interactive/2026/feb/15/cuba-crisis-oil-shortage-venezuela-donald-trump-havana">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>Venezuelan deportee can return to US but fears repeat of ordeal: ‘I’m not over that nightmare yet’</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/14/venezuelan-illegally-deported-return-fears</link>
      <description><p>Luis Muñoz Pinto, 27, who was sent to notoriously brutal prison in El Salvador, would like to clear his name after US judge’s ruling</p><p>A US federal judge’s order that some of the Venezuelan men sent by the Trump administration to a notorious prison in El Salvador must be allowed to return to the United States to fight their cases has been greeted with hope and a sense of vindication – but also fear – by one of the deportees.<br><br>
 US district judge James Boasberg <a href="https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/judge-orders-return-venezuelans-formerly-detained-el-salvador-choose-c-rcna258755">ruled</a> on Thursday in Washington DC that the Trump administration should facilitate the return of deportees who are currently in countries outside <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/venezuela">Venezuela</a>, saying they must be given the opportunity to seek the due process they were denied after being illegally <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/mar/16/deportation-alleged-gang-members-el-salvador">expelled from the US</a> last March.</p><p>Boasberg added that the US government should cover the travel costs of those who wish to come to the US to argue their <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/usimmigration">immigration</a> cases.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/14/venezuelan-illegally-deported-return-fears">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/feb/14/us-military-anthropic-ai-model-claude-venezuela-raid</link>
      <description><p>Wall Street Journal says Claude used in operation via Anthropic’s partnership with Palantir Technologies</p><p>Claude, the AI model developed by <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/feb/12/anthropic-funding-round">Anthropic</a>, was used by the US military during its operation to kidnap Nicolás Maduro from Venezuela, the Wall Street Journal <a href="https://www.wsj.com/politics/national-security/pentagon-used-anthropics-claude-in-maduro-venezuela-raid-583aff17">revealed</a> on Saturday, a high-profile example of how the US defence department is using artificial intelligence in its operations.</p><p>The US raid on Venezuela involved bombing across the capital, Caracas, and the killing of 83 people, according to Venezuela’s defence ministry. Anthropic’s terms of use prohibit the use of Claude for violent ends, for the development of weapons or for conducting surveillance.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/feb/14/us-military-anthropic-ai-model-claude-venezuela-raid">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Worst weather forecast to hit late on Sunday, a day after floods caused power outages, road collapses and home evacuations</p><p>New Zealand’s weather bureau has warned more flooding could hit the country’s North Island, a day after floods caused power outages, road collapses, home evacuations and caused the death of a man whose vehicle was submerged on a highway.</p><p>There was “threat to life from dangerous river conditions, significant flooding and slips” as a deepening low-pressure system east of the North Island brought heavy rain and severe gales to several regions, the weather bureau said.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/15/new-zealand-officials-warn-more-flooding-could-hit-north-island-as-man-killed-after-heavy-rain">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Defence leaders write joint appeal urging public on need to be prepared for war with Russia and resulting costs</p><ul><li><p><a href="https://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/2026/feb/15/defence-chiefs-russia-rearmament-security-britain-germany-europe">As defence chiefs, we must warn you about Russia and say this: rearmament is not warmongering | Richard Knighton and Carsten Breuer</a></p></li></ul><p>Britain and Germany’s highest ranking military leaders have made an unprecedented joint appeal to the public to accept the “moral” case for rearmament and prepare for the threat of war with Russia.</p><p>The pair said they were making the plea not just as the military leaders of two of Europe’s largest military spenders, but “as voices for a Europe that must now confront uncomfortable truths about its security”.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2026/feb/15/top-british-and-german-military-chiefs-press-moral-case-for-rearmament">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Yvette Cooper says claim against Kremlin ‘deeply serious’ while Russia dismisses western ‘feeblemindedness’</p><p>The UK is mulling fresh sanctions against Moscow after <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/14/alexei-navalny-poisoning-death-russia-frog-toxin">pinning blame on the Kremlin</a> for the poisoning of the Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny, Yvette Cooper has suggested.</p><p>The Foreign Office and four of the UK’s allies – Sweden, France, Germany and the Netherlands – announced on Saturday they had determined that Navalny’s death was most likely the result of poisoning using dart frog toxin arranged by the Russian state.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2026/feb/15/uk-considers-new-russia-sanctions-navalny-frog-toxin">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <guid>https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2026/feb/15/uk-considers-new-russia-sanctions-navalny-frog-toxin</guid>
      <media:content width="140" url="https://i.guim.co.uk/img/media/cfa11b8b9508bc8551729e93228c2da241ba4de4/597_144_2554_2043/master/2554.jpg?width=140&quality=85&auto=format&fit=max&s=a6e853cdea43d8f397ff12c9d3fb934b">
        <media:credit scheme="urn:ebu">Photograph: Stefan Rousseau/PA</media:credit>
      </media:content>
      <media:content width="460" url="https://i.guim.co.uk/img/media/cfa11b8b9508bc8551729e93228c2da241ba4de4/597_144_2554_2043/master/2554.jpg?width=460&quality=85&auto=format&fit=max&s=3d1c0fa91b9ef492857ca5c42347169a">

      <title>Offer to join Trump’s new era is met with growing sense of European steeliness</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/15/offer-to-join-trumps-new-era-is-met-with-growing-sense-of-european-steeliness</link>
      <description><p>Talk of a stronger, independent Europe was the dominant mood in Munich amid bitter disagreement on Ukraine</p><p>If JD Vance’s thuggish speech to last year’s Munich Security Conference, directed at the solar plexus of Europe, marked the moment when a transatlantic breakup started, this weekend’s conference, in a rainy and cold Bavaria, was where the debate about the terms of the divorce settlement got under way.</p><p>Marco Rubio, the chosen Washington representative this year, is a diplomat, so he softened the Trumpian tone with references to German beer, the Beatles, Dante and the Mayflower. But his speech was a stern warning that if Europe wanted to continue on its path of civilisational decline, as this US administration sees it, America would not be interested and has different hemispheres on which to focus.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/15/offer-to-join-trumps-new-era-is-met-with-growing-sense-of-european-steeliness">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/live/2026/feb/15/munich-security-conference-rubio-kallas-lagarde-europe-latest-news-updates</link>
      <description><p>EU’s foreign policy chief says many countries still ‘want to join our club’</p><p>EU’s <strong>Kallas</strong> appears to be slightly sceptical about the idea of appointing an EU envoy for talks on ending the Russian invasion of Ukraine.</p><p>She earlier said that “what matters more than having a seat at the table is knowing what to ask [for] when you are sitting there.”</p><p><em>“That’s why I proposed to the member states [a] concrete mandate [of] the asks that we would have to Russia. <strong>So whoever goes to that table, whether it’s individually or bilaterally, they should ask [for] these things from the Russians.</strong></em></p><p><em>We have a saying in Estonian that if you demand a lot, you get little; if you demand little, you get nothing, and if you demand nothing, you pay on top.”</em></p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/live/2026/feb/15/munich-security-conference-rubio-kallas-lagarde-europe-latest-news-updates">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Concern grows over Narges Mohammadi’s health, family says, after reports of ‘life-threatening mistreatment’</p><p>Iranian authorities have without prior warning transferred Nobel peace prize laureate Narges Mohammadi to a prison in the north of the country as concern grows over her health, her family said on Saturday.</p><p>Mohammadi, who won the peace prize in 2023 in recognition for more than two decades of campaigning, was arrested on 12 December in the eastern city of Mashhad after speaking out against Iran’s clerical authorities at a funeral ceremony.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/14/nobel-laureate-transferred-to-prison-in-northern-iran-without-warning">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>USS Gerald R Ford will take about three weeks to sail to region, amid push for Iran to curb its nuclear ambitions</p><p>Donald Trump has ordered the world’s largest aircraft carrier to sail from the Caribbean Sea to the Middle East in an effort to increase pressure on Iran amid discussions over curbing its nuclear and ballistic missile programmes.</p><p>The USS Gerald R Ford and its supporting warships should take about three weeks to return to the region, where they will join the USS Abraham Lincoln, dramatically increasing the military firepower available to the US leader.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/13/trump-sends-second-aircraft-carrier-middle-east-iran">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Mohan Karki – one of many people ICE has deported to countries with which they have little connection – leaves behind his wife and seven-month-old baby he has yet to hold</p><p>Tika Basnet sat facing the glow of her iPhone, a red <em>tika</em> pressed into the center of her forehead. Seven-month-old Briana slept on her lap, her breathing soft and uneven. On the other side of the screen was Mohan Karki, Basnet’s husband, who had yet to hold his daughter.</p><p>For Karki, nearly 9,000 miles (14,500km) away, it was already morning. He was in hiding in south Asia, his exact location withheld for his safety, his face breaking into pixels as he watched his daughter sleep.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/15/bhutanese-nepali-refugee-ice-trump-immigration">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/13/bangladesh-election-bnp-wins</link>
      <description><p>Voting was largely peaceful in an election seen as a test of Bangladesh’s democracy after years of political turmoil </p><p>The Bangladesh Nationalist party, led by <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/12/tarique-rahman-bnp-bangladesh-election">Tarique Rahman</a>, has won a sweeping victory in the country’s first election since a gen Z uprising toppled the autocratic regime of <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/sheikh-hasina">Sheikh Hasina</a>.</p><p>Results from the election commission confirmed the BNP alliance had won 212 seats, returning the party to power after 20 years, while the rival alliance, led by the Islamist party Jamaat-e-Islami, won 77 seats.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/13/bangladesh-election-bnp-wins">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>‘Invisible’ children born in the brothels of Bangladesh finally get birth certificates</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/global-development/2026/feb/13/invisible-children-born-in-the-brothels-of-bangladesh-finally-get-birth-certificates</link>
      <description><p>Destined to a perilous life with no right to an education or to vote, state recognition ‘gives them hope’, campaigners say</p><p>Through the decades that <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/video/2016/may/17/children-trapped-bangladesh-brothel-village-video?CMP=share_btn_tw">the Daulatdia brothel</a> in Bangladesh has existed, children born there have been invisible, unable to be registered because their mothers were sex workers and their fathers unknown. Now, for the first time, all 400 of them in the brothel village have their own birth certificates.</p><p>That milestone was reached after a push by campaigners who have spent decades working with Bangladesh’s undocumented children born in brothels or on the street. It means they can finally access the rights afforded to other citizens: the ability to go to school, to be issued a passport or to vote.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/global-development/2026/feb/13/invisible-children-born-in-the-brothels-of-bangladesh-finally-get-birth-certificates">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/12/tarique-rahman-bnp-bangladesh-election</link>
      <description><p>Jailed then exiled in London, Rahman returns home as the main contender to be next prime minister of Bangladesh</p><p>Tarique Rahman, who after 17 years in exile is the main contender to be the next prime minister of Bangladesh, has pledged to end entrenched corruption and put the country on a “new path” as voting began in the first free and fair elections in almost two decades.</p><p>Speaking to the Guardian before polls opened on Thursday morning, Rahman promised a new era of clean politics, including a “top down, no tolerance” approach to graft, if his Bangladesh Nationalist party (BNP) was brought to power.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/12/tarique-rahman-bnp-bangladesh-election">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>After her death, police dropped a case examining Katie Madden’s former partner, despite evidence he had repeatedly told her to kill herself</p><ul><li><p><a href="https://www.theguardian.com/society/2026/feb/15/number-uk-women-suicide-domestic-abuse-under-reported-say-experts">Revealed: The true toll of female suicides in UK with domestic abuse at their core</a></p></li></ul><p>Hours before Katie Madden took her own life, she had a tense phone call with her former partner Jonathon Russell. Russell was on bail after allegedly assaulting Madden – he was banned from contacting her – but the conversation took place nevertheless.</p><p>There was a witness to the call who gave evidence to the inquest into Madden’s death.       <link>https://www.theguardian.com/society/2026/feb/15/measles-outbreak-under-10s-north-london</link>

      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/15/nancy-guthrie-missing-third-week</link>
      <description><p>Authorities await DNA test results from pair of gloves found near home as search continues to draw national attention</p><p>The search for Nancy Guthrie, the 84-year-old mother of NBC’s Today show anchor Savannah Guthrie, has entered its third week, as investigators ask neighbors within a two-mile radius to share home video footage and authorities await DNA test results from a pair of gloves found near the home.</p><p>Nancy Guthrie was last seen on the evening of 31 January, when she was dropped off at her home in the Catalina foothills north of Tucson, <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/arizona">Arizona</a>, after having dinner with her older daughter and son-in-law. She was <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/02/savannah-guthrie-mother-missing-arizona">reported missing</a> the following day, after she failed to arrive at a friend’s house to watch a church service.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/15/nancy-guthrie-missing-third-week">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Ofelia Torres, 16, spotlighted her dad Ruben’s illegal detention last fall during Trump’s crackdown in Chicago</p><p>A <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/chicago">Chicago</a> teenager, whose father was detained by <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/usimmigration">immigration</a> authorities while she navigated cancer, died on Friday, a family spokesperson said.</p><p>Ofelia Torres, a 16-year-old in Chicago, had been undergoing treatment for an aggressive and rare form of cancer since late 2024. As she and her family struggled with the medical procedures, her father, Ruben Torres Maldonado, was detained by immigration authorities while at a Home Depot in October, leading to a contentious and public case that highlighted the human effects of the <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/trump-administration">Trump administration</a>’s aggressive <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/oct/03/my-travels-through-chicago-tear-gas-resistance-and-trumps-big-immigration-crackdown">immigration crackdown</a>.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/15/chicago-ofelia-torres-death-cancer-father-ice-custody">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Real estate agent brothers Tal, Oren and Alon Alexander – known as ‘closers’ – are on trial in New York for sex trafficking</p><p></p><p>In their time as real estate brokers, the Israeli-American Alexander brothers – twins Alon and Oren and older brother Tal – were known as “closers”, the salesmen who could a get a sale over finish line, often to wealthy hedge funders who were then making hay in the aftermath of the 2008 financial crisis.</p><p>Their technique, one real estate expert explained outside the 26th floor of the federal court house in lower Manhattan last week, was based on the sense that the property salesmen “were just like their clients” – young, eager and successful.       <title>Progressive Texas organizers hail shock win as far-right Republicans left reeling</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/15/texas-organizers-republicans</link>
      <description><p>Elation as anti-extremists fight back against influence of billionaire megadonors through grassroots organizing</p><p>Chris Tackett started tracking extremism in Texas politics about a decade ago, whenever his schedule as a Little League coach and school board member would allow. At the time, he lived in Granbury, 40 minutes west of Fort Worth. He’d noticed that a local member of the state legislature, Mike Lang, had become a vocal advocate for <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/may/03/texas-schools-greg-abbott-vouchers">using public money for private schools</a> – despite the fact that Lang campaigned as a supporter of public education.</p><p>With a little research, Tackett found that Lang had received hundreds of thousands of dollars in campaign donations from the Wilks brothers and Tim Dunn, billionaire megadonors whose deep pockets and Christian nationalist views have <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2023/sep/05/texas-fracking-billionaire-brothers-prageru-daily-wire">consumed the Texas GOP</a>. Tackett published his findings on social media, and soon enough, people started asking him to create pie charts of their representatives’ campaign funds. These charts evolved into the organisation <a href="https://seeitnameitfightit.com/">See It. Name It. Fight It</a>.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/feb/15/texas-organizers-republicans">Continue reading...</a></description>

      </media:content>
      <media:content width="460" url="https://i.guim.co.uk/img/media/015e4e28ebf320488a18f09033f0a928d225e725/91_0_1403_1122/master/1403.jpg?width=460&quality=85&auto=format&fit=max&s=864882a83939e4743ec8bf3593999e76">
